card.intuition: A specific type of message queue used to distribute unit-of-work 'jobs' across multiple worker processes to be executed in the background.
card.analogy: A kitchen ticket rail: the server (Producer) clips a ticket ('Order: 2 Burgers') to the rail. The line cooks (Workers) each grab one ticket at a time, finish the job, and then take the next one.
when_to_use: Generating PDF reports or processing large CSV files
Web scraping or long-running data syncs
gotchas: Ensure your jobs are 'Idempotent' — if a worker crashes halfway through and the job is retried, it shouldn't cause double-billing or corruption
Monitoring: you always need to watch the 'Queue Depth' to know if you need to add more worker servers

exercise.prompt: Identify the popular library for Job Queues in these languages.
exercise.test_code:
assert libraries["Python"].lower() == "celery"
assert libraries["JavaScript/Node"].lower() in ["bull", "bullmq"]
print("PASS")
